sources.notes: Read the complete current statement, constraints, path definition, modulus, and examples. The current revised statement contains one test case with n<=2e5 and a_i<=1e6.

Read the official 2037G analysis and code. It derives graph-path DP accelerated by inclusion-exclusion over distinct prime-factor subsets and also identifies a Mobius-function alternative. The displayed code retains an old outer test-case loop that does not match the current single-case statement; only the algorithmic content was used.

Read the complete independent explanation and implementation. It confirms distinct-prime factorization, subset-product inclusion-exclusion, dp[1]=1, and updating divisor buckets after computing each state. The prose reverses one divisibility relation, while its code uses the required bucket meaning d divides a_j.

Read the full independent Mobius derivation. It expresses the non-coprime predecessor sum as total prior dp minus the Mobius-weighted coprime sum and gives an O(V+n*max tau(a_i)) all-divisor implementation.

Re-derived the subset coefficient for a predecessor sharing r primes as sum_{s=1..r}(-1)^(s+1)C(r,s)=1. Exhaustively compared the optimized recurrence with O(n^2) graph DP for every array of length at most 6 with values from 2 through 12; all answers matched.

annotation.summary: Count directed paths from city 1 to city n, where an edge i->j exists exactly when i<j and gcd(a_i,a_j)>1, modulo 998244353.
annotation.constraints: The current statement has one test case; 2 <= n <= 2*10^5; 2 <= a_i <= 10^6; the answer is taken modulo 998244353.
annotation.core_idea: Indices already topologically order the graph. Let dp[1]=1 and dp[i]=sum dp[j] over j<i with gcd(a_j,a_i)>1. Maintain bucket[d]=sum of dp[j] over processed cities whose a_j is divisible by squarefree d. Factor a_i into its distinct primes P. The valid predecessors are the union of the sets sharing each p in P, so apply inclusion-exclusion: dp[i]=sum over nonempty S subseteq P of (-1)^(|S|+1)*bucket[product(S)]. A predecessor sharing r distinct primes is counted with coefficient sum_{s=1..r}(-1)^(s+1)C(r,s)=1, while a coprime predecessor is counted zero times. After computing dp[i], add it to bucket[product(S)] for every nonempty subset S, preventing self-counting. Since a_i<=1e6, it has at most seven distinct prime factors, so each city needs at most 127 subset terms.
annotation.complexity: With a smallest-prime-factor sieve up to A=max(a_i), O(A log log A + sum_i 2^omega(a_i)) time, bounded by O(A log log A + 128n), and O(A) memory.
tricks: Use the natural index order as a topological order for the implicit DAG.
Aggregate previous dp values by divisibility instead of testing all predecessors.
Discard prime exponents because gcd>1 only asks whether at least one prime is shared.
Apply inclusion-exclusion over nonempty subsets of distinct prime factors.
Update divisor buckets only after computing dp[i] so a city cannot transition to itself.

solution_variants.name: distinct-prime-subset-inclusion-exclusion
solution_variants.summary: Maintain dp sums for squarefree products of distinct primes. Query the union of shared-prime predecessor sets by alternating over all nonempty prime subsets, then add the new dp value to the same buckets.
solution_variants.complexity: O(A log log A + sum_i 2^omega(a_i)) time and O(A) memory

solution_variants.name: mobius-over-all-divisors
solution_variants.summary: Maintain total prior path count and bucket[d]=sum dp[j] for all processed values divisible by d. Using [gcd(x,y)=1]=sum_{d|gcd(x,y)}mu(d), compute dp[i]=total-sum_{d|a_i}mu(d)*bucket[d], then update every divisor of a_i.
solution_variants.complexity: O(A + sum_i tau(a_i)) time with a linear Mobius sieve and O(A) memory

tags.evidence: Each state dp[i] is the number of paths ending at graph vertex i, and transitions aggregate path counts from earlier adjacent vertices in the implicit DAG.
The requested answer is exactly the number of directed paths from city 1 to city n, with increasing indices making every visited set correspond to one ordered path.
Predecessors sharing several prime factors occur in several divisibility buckets; alternating over prime-factor subsets makes every non-coprime predecessor contribute exactly once.
Every a_i is reduced to its set of distinct prime factors, whose subset products index all required aggregate buckets.
All nonempty subsets of at most seven distinct prime factors are enumerated by masks to build products and determine inclusion-exclusion signs.
The alternative computes the weighted sum of coprime predecessors using the Mobius identity over common divisors and subtracts it from the total prior path count.
